Opportunity Name: Call for Proposals: Journalism Partnerships – Collaborations (2026)
Funding/Salary/Benefit: Up to €2 million per project (max), co-financing up to 80% of eligible costs.
Summary: Supports cross-border media organisations to come together and develop new business models, newsroom types, technical solutions (platforms, formats, syndication networks).
Target Sector/Beneficiaries: Media organisations developing product/technical solutions, new formats, business models
Eligibility: Consortia of media organisations (press, radio, TV, online) and allied others (NGOs, media associations).
Consortium/Partnership Notes: Yes – Minimum 3 partners from 3 different countries in Creative Europe Programme.
End Summary: Excellent match for service-design or product development in media (platforms, syndication, technical standards).

Opportunity Name: Call for Proposals: Journalism Partnerships – Collaborations (2026)
Funding/Salary/Benefit: Up to €2 million per project (max), co-financing up to 80% of eligible costs.
Summary: Supports cross-border media organisations to come together and develop new business models, newsroom types, technical solutions (platforms, formats, syndication networks).
Target Sector/Beneficiaries: Media organisations developing product/technical solutions, new formats, business models
Eligibility: Consortia of media organisations (press, radio, TV, online) and allied others (NGOs, media associations).
Consortium/Partnership Notes: Yes – Minimum 3 partners from 3 different countries in Creative Europe Programme.
End Summary: Excellent match for service-design or product development in media (platforms, syndication, technical standards).

Summary: Accelerator for media entrepreneurs focusing on business/product innovation in local journalism.
Eligibility: Media entrepreneurs and journalism startups
End Summary: Business-focused product development for media orgs.

Sponsoring Organization/Funder: News Product Alliance in partnership with Craig Newmark Graduate School of Journalism at CUNY
Funding/Salary/Benefit: Tuition: USD 5,500(for the 2025 cohort). Limited scholarships and organizational sponsorships may be available.
Summary: A 3.5-month, part-time, virtual cohort-based program designed for mid- to senior-level news professionals to develop leadership, strategy, and technical skills in news product management and innovation.
Eligibility: Open to individuals with mid- to senior-level experience in news, media, or related product roles. Applicants must have professional experience; entry-level candidates are not eligible.
Consortium/Partnership Notes: Individual applicants only. News organizations may secure up to two seats for employees but consortium bids are not accepted.
End Summary: A globally recognized certification program that strengthens the leadership pipeline in news product management, combining hands-on learning, a capstone project, and an international alumni network.

Sponsoring Organization/Funder: Wallace House Center for Journalists / University of Michigan
Summary: Year-long fully funded fellowship for mid-career journalists to pursue academic study and professional development.
Eligibility: Journalists with at least five years of experience in news organizations or as freelancers.
End Summary: Prestigious U.S.-based fellowship providing sabbatical and research opportunities for journalists.

Sponsoring Organization/Funder: NEWSWELL at Arizona State University (ASU) in partnership with the Knight Center for the Future of News
Funding/Salary/Benefit: No registration fee. Participants are responsible for their own travel and accommodation expenses.
Summary: A three-day, invitation-only accelerator and convening that brings together journalists, technologists, and AI leaders to explore the role of artificial intelligence in transforming journalism, from reporting and production to business sustainability.
Application Deadline: No registration fee. Participants are responsible for their own travel and accommodation expenses.
Eligibility: Open to individuals — journalists, media technologists, and AI professionals. Participation is by nomination or competitive application only. Limited to approximately 100 attendees.
Geographic Focus: United States (national focus, with some international participation possible)
Consortium/Partnership Notes: Not a consortium model. Individual nominations or invitations only; no joint or group applications.
End Summary: A forward-looking program hosted by ASU NEWSWELL and Knight Center to shape responsible and innovative uses of AI in journalism, offering collaboration and mentorship among leading industry experts.

Opportunity Name: Coalition for Innovative Media Measurement (CIMM) Startup Program
Funding/Salary/Benefit: No direct cash; benefits include mentorship, industry exposure, and access to CIMM member network.
Summary: One-year programme selecting five early-stage companies focused on *product/solution innovation* in media measurement, data, and analytics.
Eligibility: Early-stage startups (under 3 years) developing measurement/analytics/tech solutions; focus on product-development.
Consortium/Partnership Notes: Partnerships with CIMM members expected; cohort encourages collaboration.
End Summary: Good fit for product design and solution development in media measurement/analytics.

Opportunity Name: Marco Castro Cosio Media Art & Technology Fellowship
Sponsoring Organization/Funder: Brown Institute for Media Innovation at Columbia Journalism School
Funding/Salary/Benefit: USD 10,000 grant + optional residency at Brown Institute
Summary: Fellowship for creators working at the intersection of journalism, media, and technology to prototype products, tools, or art-tech experiments that reimagine media.
Target Sector/Beneficiaries: Media innovation, product development, design technology
Eligibility: Professionals from any field (media, tech, arts, design) developing a product or prototype relevant to media innovation
Consortium/Partnership Notes: Interdisciplinary collaboration encouraged; no formal consortium needed
End Summary: Strongly aligns with journalism product and design innovation.

Opportunity Name: Nieman-Berkman Klein Fellowship in Journalism Innovation
Sponsoring Organization/Funder: Nieman Foundation for Journalism & Berkman Klein Center for Internet & Society (Harvard University)
Funding/Salary/Benefit: Approx. USD 85,000 stipend for 9 months + access to Harvard resources
Summary: A year-long fellowship for journalists or media professionals developing tools, products, or design-led innovations that advance the future of journalism. Fellows spend an academic year at Harvard developing their projects and collaborating with peers.
Application Deadline: International applicants: Dec 1, 2025; U.S. citizens: Jan 31, 2026
Target Sector/Beneficiaries: Journalism innovation, product design, newsroom tools
Eligibility: Mid-career journalists or professionals (including freelancers) focused on technology, business, or design innovation in media
Consortium/Partnership Notes: Individual or collaborative work encouraged; no consortium requirement
End Summary: Focused on product/service innovation for journalism rather than traditional reporting.

Funding/Salary/Benefit: No fixed grant; participants receive accelerator support, mentorship, and exposure at IBC 2026.
Summary: A global accelerator program inviting collaborative media and entertainment organisations to co-develop innovative tools, workflows, and technologies that improve media products and distribution. Ideal for projects exploring product development or new services in media.
Eligibility: Open to teams from startups, vendors, broadcasters, or media organisations developing innovative solutions.
End Summary: Offers global collaboration opportunities for journalists and media technologists focusing on innovation and digital transformation.

Opportunity Name: Coalition for Innovative Media Measurement (CIMM) Startup Program
Funding/Salary/Benefit: No direct cash; benefits include mentorship, industry exposure, and access to CIMM member network.
Summary: One-year programme selecting five early-stage companies focused on *product/solution innovation* in media measurement, data, and analytics.
Eligibility: Early-stage startups (under 3 years) developing measurement/analytics/tech solutions; focus on product-development.
Consortium/Partnership Notes: Partnerships with CIMM members expected; cohort encourages collaboration.
End Summary: Good fit for product design and solution development in media measurement/analytics.

Funding/Salary/Benefit: No fixed grant; participants receive accelerator support, mentorship, and exposure at IBC 2026.
Summary: A global accelerator program inviting collaborative media and entertainment organisations to co-develop innovative tools, workflows, and technologies that improve media products and distribution. Ideal for projects exploring product development or new services in media.
Eligibility: Open to teams from startups, vendors, broadcasters, or media organisations developing innovative solutions.
End Summary: Offers global collaboration opportunities for journalists and media technologists focusing on innovation and digital transformation.

Summary: A four-month incubator program (Jan–Apr 2026) supporting BIPOC media entrepreneurs with coaching, training, and funding to explore storytelling innovation, tech integration, and sustainability.
Eligibility: BIPOC media founders and news-focused content creators in Philadelphia
End Summary: Designed for early-stage BIPOC-led media startups exploring innovation and product development.

Summary: Offers grants to creators and independent journalists to support collaborative projects that serve Philadelphia communities through impactful journalism and community engagement.
Eligibility: Independent journalists, content creators, and nonprofit media organizations serving Philadelphia audiences.
Consortium/Partnership Notes: Collaborations are encouraged but not required
End Summary: Supports community-rooted journalism with funding for collaborative design/engagement projects.

Funding/Salary/Benefit: Competitive salary, benefits, health insurance, relocation allowance, housing stipend
Summary: A one-year full-time program for aspiring journalists specializing in data visualization, offering immersive training and experience with top Hearst newspapers. Fellows work on digital storytelling projects, data graphics, and newsroom collaboration.
Eligibility: Open to recent graduates or those with experience/interest in journalism, data, graphics, or digital development; must be willing to relocate to San Francisco Bay Area
End Summary: Fellows will be embedded with Hearst’s DevHub team, gaining real-world experience with impactful digital journalism and data storytelling.

Funding/Salary/Benefit: $85,000 stipend (in 9.5 monthly installments); travel & housing stipend; basic health insurance for fellow and family
Summary: A competitive 9-month residential fellowship 10 full-time science journalists from around the globe, to explore science, technology, and the craft of journalism; to concentrate on a specialty in science; and to learn at some of the top research universities in the world.
Target Sector/Beneficiaries: Science, health, environment, and tech journalists, including writers, editors, producers, filmmakers
Eligibility: Full-time journalists (freelance or staff) with 3+ years covering science, tech, health, or environment; part-time writers/producers and those with a recent long fellowship (past 2 years) not eligible
End Summary: The program gives fellows a research project that draws on MIT’s resources and the greater Boston area. It honors journalists globally who show exceptional achievement and a sustained commitment to journalism.

Summary: Eight-month remote fellowships supporting individuals or institutions to develop free, open-source tools, guides, or platforms that address gaps in journalism and serve community-centered newsrooms.
Target Sector/Beneficiaries: Journalists, developers, news organizations, civic tech innovators
Eligibility: Open to journalists, researchers, developers, consultants, or organizations worldwide with a proposal for an innovative journalism-supporting product; remote with visits to RJI
End Summary: Fellows build and launch impactful tools or resources tested during the fellowship and made publicly available for journalists and newsrooms.

Summary: Supports original, data-driven journalism projects on underreported issues. Welcomes investigative stories using techniques like ML, satellite imagery, or spatial data analysis. Encourages collaboration and cross-border data projects with strong visual components.
Eligibility: Open to independent journalists and newsrooms globally. Must include detailed methodology and publication plan.
End Summary: Funding for data-rich journalism that investigates critical global issues using cutting-edge techniques.

Sponsoring Organization/Funder: JournalismAI (LSE) with support from Google News Initiative
Summary: A 9-month program supporting small to mid-sized news organizations to develop AI-powered solutions for improving audience engagement and revenue strategies. It focuses on product and service innovation using AI, not editorial or storytelling tools.
Eligibility: Small/medium news publishers (10–200 staff), 12+ months operational, digital presence, not government- or politically-affiliated. Must focus on AI use for audience/revenue, not content production.
Geographic Focus: Asia-Pacific, Europe, Middle East, Africa, USA, Latin America (specific countries listed)
Consortium/Partnership Notes: No consortium required, but collaboration across selected grantees encouraged
End Summary: This challenge empowers news publishers to build AI-powered tools and systems that support audience and business development, with a strong focus on sustainable innovation rather than content creation.

Summary: A year-end online event to review 2024 innovations and developments in product and tech within news media.
Target Sector/Beneficiaries: Media organizations and professionals in product and tech
Eligibility: INMA members; media professionals in product/tech roles
End Summary: A reflective session to assess key tech trends and media product strategies of 2024.

Opportunity Name: Agentic AI for News Media Master Class
Summary: A multi-session virtual master class focusing on agentic AI systems and how media can use them to build better products.
Target Sector/Beneficiaries: Media professionals building AI-driven news products
Eligibility: Media product, tech and editorial teams; INMA membership recommended
End Summary: Offers deep dives into AI-powered product strategies for newsrooms.

Summary: A full-time, year-long fellowship embedding journalists in top newsrooms to report on artificial intelligence. Includes a 10-week AI journalism training course, a Bay Area summit, and professional mentorship.
Target Sector/Beneficiaries: Journalists covering AI and tech’s societal impacts
Eligibility: Journalists with interest or experience in AI; global applicants; Senior Fellow roles for those with 5+ years in journalism/AI
End Summary: Combines newsroom placement with deep training on AI issues to prepare journalists for high-impact tech coverage.

Summary: Supports social media promotions and audience engagement for The Waco Bridge, a local news project.
Eligibility: College students (enrolled undergrad/grad/community college); must work from Waco, TX.
End Summary: Fellowship focuses on audience building, social media and community events.

Summary: Supports cross-platform story packaging, newsletters, explainers, TikTok/Reddit content and audience engagement.
Eligibility: Journalism students with multimedia, copywriting and platform skills
End Summary: Fellowship emphasizes user engagement and multimedia storytelling.

Summary: Involves creating data-driven stories and visual journalism using JavaScript, Illustrator, Figma, Python, R etc.
Eligibility: College students with skills in coding, graphics, data analysis
End Summary: Strong focus on data and design for visual journalism.

Summary: Builds AI/ML tools and explores use of emerging tech in newsrooms. Collaborates across editorial and technical teams.
Eligibility: Students with Python, Git, data management; AI/ML experience a plus
End Summary: Direct application of AI/ML in newsroom product development.

Summary: Supports planning and execution of in-person and virtual editorial events. Involves logistics, marketing support, outreach and coordination.
Eligibility: Students interested in event planning, marketing, journalism
End Summary: Great for students interested in event-based public engagement.

Summary: Focuses on user research, prototyping, usability testing, feature prioritization and developing product ideas for the newsroom.
Target Sector/Beneficiaries: Media product teams, UX and service designers
Eligibility: Students in UX, research, journalism innovation, product design
End Summary: Key role for those passionate about product and and innovation in journalism.

Summary: Assists with brand marketing, communications campaigns, media kits, social strategy and internal newsletters.
Eligibility: Students in marketing, PR, journalism or communication fields
End Summary: A hybrid communication-marketing fellowship supporting newsroom growth.

Opportunity Name: Call for applications: Media Consultants – Media Innovation & Sustainability programme
Summary: IPI is seeking experienced media professionals to act as consultants for their Media Innovation & Sustainability programme, supporting independent newsrooms in areas such as news-product development, user research, and innovation strategy.
Eligibility: Open to experienced media professionals with expertise in journalism innovation and product development
Consortium/Partnership Notes: Not specified; designed for individual consultants supporting media orgs
End Summary: This opportunity is ideal for professionals in service-design or media product strategy aiming to support journalism innovation and sustainability.

Funding/Salary/Benefit: Non-monetary benefit – mentorship support, professional development, and network building
Summary: A mentorship programme matching media innovators and journalists with seasoned professionals to enhance skills in media management, product development, audience engagement, and more, to support media innovation. Applicants can apply for one of these tracks as a 1) mentor, 2) mentee or 3) peer mentor, please follow these steps.
Target Sector/Beneficiaries: Journalists, media innovators, small and mid-sized media organizations
Eligibility: Individual journalists and media professionals or newsroom teams based in Europe. Applicants must be working on a media innovation project or in the early stages of one.
Geographic Focus: Europe (applicants must be based in European countries)
End Summary: Rolling mentorship opportunity offering tailored expert guidance to European journalists and media innovators. By pairing them with experienced mentors, the initiative supports the development and scaling of innovative media projects, helping participants build more resilient and audience-focused media organizations.

Sponsoring Organization/Funder: Public Media Alliance / Grace Wyndham Goldie (BBC) Trust Fund
Summary: Supports international travel (excluding UK) for staff of PMA-member public media organisations for ~1–2 weeks to undertake a media/research project, skills development, or capacity-building initiative.
Target Sector/Beneficiaries: Journalists, editors, media professionals at PMA-member organisations
Eligibility: Must be employed at a PMA-member public media organisation for at least 2 years; mid-career professionals; travel to the UK is not allowed
Consortium/Partnership Notes: Not required; host organisation must be a PMA member
End Summary: Enables international collaboration and knowledge exchange among public media professionals

Sponsoring Organization/Funder: Public Media Alliance / Grace Wyndham Goldie (BBC) Trust Fund
Funding/Salary/Benefit: Grant covers trainer costs (exact amount not specified)
Summary: Funds workshops or training events by bringing an international trainer to a PMA-member organisation, focusing on issues like misinformation, safety, AI in the newsroom, etc.
Eligibility: Only PMA-member organisations can apply; must propose relevant training need and identify a qualified trainer
Consortium/Partnership Notes: Individual PMA-member organisations apply to host trainers; no consortiums required
End Summary: Supports tailored professional training within public media organisations to build institutional capacity
